verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: You have desired, beloved Fortunatus that, since the burden of persecutions and afflictions is lying heavy upon us, and in the ending and completion of the world the hateful time of Antichrist is already beginning to draw near, I would collect from the sacred Scriptures some exhortations for preparing and strengthening the minds of the brethren, whereby I might animate the soldiers of Christ for the heavenly and spiritual contest.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: I have been constrained to obey your so needful wish, so that as much as my limited powers, instructed by the aid of divine inspiration, are sufficient, some arms, as it were, and defences might be brought forth from the Lord’s precepts for the brethren who are about to fight.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: For it is little to arouse God’s people by the trumpet call of our voice, unless we confirm the faith of believers, and their valour dedicated and devoted to God, by the divine readings.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: But what more fitly or more fully agrees with my own care and solicitude, than to prepare the people divinely entrusted to me, and an army established in the heavenly camp, by assiduous exhortations against the darts and weapons of the devil?

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: For he cannot be a soldier fitted for the war who has not first been exercised in the field; nor will he who seeks to gain the crown of contest be rewarded on the racecourse, unless he first considers the use and skilfulness of his powers.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: It is an ancient adversary and an old enemy with whom we wage our battle: six thousand years are now nearly completed since the devil first attacked man.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: All kinds of temptation, and arts, and snares for his overthrow, he has learned by the very practice of long years.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: If he finds Christ’s soldier unprepared, if unskilled, if not careful and watching with his whole heart; he circumvents him if ignorant, he deceives him incautious, he cheats him inexperienced.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: But if a man, keeping the Lord’s precepts, and bravely adhering to Christ, stands against him, he must needs be conquered, because Christ, whom that man confesses, is unconquered.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And that I might not extend my discourse, beloved brother, to too great a length, and fatigue my hearer or reader by the abundance of a too diffuse style, I have made a compendium; so that the titles being placed first, which every one ought both to know and to have in mind, I might subjoin sections of the Lord’s word, and establish what I had proposed by the authority of the divine teaching, in such wise as that I might not appear to have sent you my own treatise so much, as to have suggested material for others to discourse on; a proceeding which will be of advantage to individuals with increased benefit.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: For if I were to give a man a garment finished and ready, it would be my garment that another was making use of, and probably the thing made for another would be found little fitting for his figure of stature and body.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: But now I have sent you the very wool and the purple from the Lamb, by whom we were redeemed and quickened; which, when you have received, you will make into a coat for yourself according to your own will, and the rather that you will rejoice in it as your own private and special garment.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And you will exhibit to others also what we have sent, that they themselves may be able to finish it according to their will; so that that old nakedness being covered, they may all bear the garments of Christ robed in the sanctification of heavenly grace.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Moreover also, beloved brethren, I have considered it a useful and wholesome plan in an exhortation so needful as that which may make martyrs, to cut off all delays and tardiness in our words, and to put away the windings of human discourse, and set down only those things which God speaks, wherewith Christ exhorts His servants to martyrdom.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Those divine precepts themselves must be supplied, as it were, for arms for the combatants.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Let them be the incitements of the warlike trumpet; let them be the clarion-blast for the warriors.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Let the ears be roused by them; let the minds be prepared by them; let the powers both of soul and body be strengthened to all endurance of suffering.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Let us only who, by the Lord’s permission, have given the first baptism to believers, also prepare each one for the second; urging and teaching that this is a baptism greater in grace, more lofty in power, more precious in honour—a baptism wherein angels baptize—a baptism in which God and His Christ exult—a baptism after which no one sins any more—a baptism which completes the increase of our faith—a baptism which, as we withdraw from the world, immediately associates us with God.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: In the baptism of water is received the remission of sins, in the baptism of blood the crown of virtues.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: This thing is to be embraced and desired, and to be asked for in all the entreaties of our petitions, that we who are God’s servants should be also His friends.
